text: The PMS shall manage a database with bibliographical information.
The PMS shall save the entire text of a publication in PDF and Postscript formats.
The PMS shall automatically retrieve bibliographical information from the complete text of a publication.
The PMS shall provide a manual interface to correct retrieved bibliographical information.
The PMS shall recognize specific authors to allow manual linking of different name variations to a single entity.
The PMS shall provide a bulk update function for importing multiple publications or abstracts simultaneously.
The PMS shall support access control for publication contents based on user privileges.
The PMS shall be accessible through a Command Line Interface (CLI).
The PMS shall be accessible through a web browser.
The system shall interface with a MySQL database.
The web interface shall comply with the W3C XHTML 1.0 Transitional standard.
The PMS shall log internal errors and notify the Administrator.
The PMS shall communicate via HTTP using TCP/IP port 80.
The PMS shall run under a Linux-based environment.
The PMS shall be developed using the Ruby programming language.
The system shall require Guest users to provide a first name, last name, password, and valid email address to register.
The PMS shall send a validation email to confirm the registration of a new user account.
The system shall allow users to search for publications by title, author, and institute.
The PMS shall recognize VUB-Network Users by their IP address.
The PMS shall allow Publishers to upload publications in PDF and PS formats following BibTeX or RIS standards.
The PMS shall allow Moderators to edit or delete departmental publications.
The PMS shall allow Administrators to upgrade, downgrade, or delete any user account.
The PMS shall provide a search function supporting logical expressions such as AND, OR, and NOT.
The PMS shall allow users to order search results by title, author, and year of publication.
The system shall allow users to export publication references in BibTeX and RIS formats.
The PMS shall encrypt user passwords during submission and storage.
The system shall maintain database consistency at all times.
The PMS shall provide a backup structure to save all stored data.
The system shall restrict direct database access to Administrators via the CLI.
The PMS shall display an error message to visitors stating the system is down during maintenance or failure.
